events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Pollution Challenge

But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show!

Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Toddler Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 11:00 to 11:30am.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 18 months, and their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play from 11:00 to 11:30.

events.title: EarlyON Family Fun Time
events.description: Join us for a fun and interactive play program. Children and their parents/caregivers can access a variety of activities to play and learn together. You will also have opportunities to connect with others or speak to staff about your child's development or parenting needs. Ages 0 to 6 years.  Limit of 30 participants

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Harry Turns 70! Celebrating Canadian Illustrators
events.description: Harry the Dirty Dog turns 70! Celebrate 70 years of a beloved classic and the illustrations behind it. This exhibit highlights the work of Margaret Bloy Graham and her ability to bring stories to life through her artwork. Visit the Osborne collection to experience our exhibit, which also spotlights the contributions of Canadian illustrators, featuring works from artists all across the country. The exhibit can be viewed during the Osborne Collection's open hours. Located on the 4th floor of

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club - Book Reporting
events.description: June 20th - August 31st. Ages 0 - 13. Summer Reading Club is here ! Register your child for the club and receive a TDSRC book reporting workbook, stickers, and more to get the most out of your summer reading. Kids will be able to report 2 books per day during the book reporting times and for  every 5 books reported they will receive an entry for a grand prize ! events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Pottery Camp with Children's Art Studio
events.description: In this fun, hands-on pottery camp, young artists will explore clay sculpture while building confidence and creative thinking in a supportive, playful environment. Participants will learn a variety of hand-building techniques, including shaping, sizing, coiling, and adding creative surface details. Presented by the Children's Art Studio. events.description: Over four days, kids ages 9-12 will work in teams to learn how to build and code LEGO Spike Robots to solve and compete in various challenges. Registration is required. Children must be between the ages of 9-12 years and able to attend ALL 4 days of the camp in order to participate. events.title: Wonder Workshop: Pollinators Challenge
events.description: Have you ever wondered at the beauty of a monarch butterfly and what they overcome to survive? What would happen if these important pollinators disappeared? Join us to learn all about the journey of the monarch butterfly. Then use your imagination to design and build your own mock butterfly garden station. Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6 to 10. Registration Required. The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library und

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children age 9+. events.title: Magic in the Moonlit Forest Camp
events.description: Step into a world of magic, mystery, and creativity! In this fun and imaginative workshop, kids will paint their very own enchanted forest using bright acrylic paints. They will explore dreamy trees, glowing paths, whimsical animals, and hidden fairy homes while learning simple painting techniques like blending, layering, and texture. Perfect for young artists of all skill levels 8-10 years old.

events.title: Bill Russell: Du pays des bayous et des prairies !
events.description: À partir de chants et histoires de la Louisiane, Bill Russell avive son jeune public. Tout le monde est invité à participer durant le spectacle. Il raconte avec un sourire dans un coin de la bouche le passé et le présent des Cajuns et leurs liens avec les Acadiens. Featuring songs and stories from Louisiana, Bill Russell enlivens his young audience. Everyone is invited to participate during the show. events.title: Kids Crafternoon

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show!

events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.description: Looking for something to do on a boring afternoon? Come out to our Summer Fun Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! Activities may include beading, mini canvas painting, perler beads, button making, and more!

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour

Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Clean Water Challenge
events.description: Have you ever wondered about the earth’s water supply? Together, we will consider how pollution is impacting the ocean and the sea creatures that live there. Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that cleans the ocean of trash and pollution. Plus, enter a random draw for a chance to win a book prize. *For kids ages 6 to 10. Space is limited.

events.title: After School Club: Storytelling Fun with Linh Nguyen
events.description: It’s easy to get bogged down with grammar and punctuation when starting out as a young writer - but holding on to the fun of storytelling is even more important! events.title: Artists in the Library: Animate with 16mm film
events.description: Children ages 9-12 please join us for one-of-a-kind workshop where you'll animate by hand on 16mm film to create your own short stop-motion piece. Participants will be taught an array of direct animation techniques to scratch, paint, draw, on a film strip. Community viewing on Saturday November 14th, 2-4 pm.

events.title: Typing Practice for Kids & Teens
events.description: Typing is a key skill for writing reports, essays and other schoolwork. This program is intended to help children and teens who struggle with typing to practice using a keyboard and learn how to type faster and with increased accuracy. Participants will learn to type together using Tux typing which is a free and open source typing tutor created especially for children. Prerequisites: No computer experience required. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Works by Asefeh Katoozian
events.description: Throughout the month of July, the works of artist Asefeh Katoozian are on display at Morningside branch. Asefeh is a multidisciplinary artist and architect with an interest in philosophy. Her work explores painting through rhythm, form, structure, and visual language, focusing on the relationship between composition and perception. She transforms visual elements into expressive forms that create a dialogue between nature, memory, and movement. Her practice reflects an ongoing exploration of how

events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Summer Camp for Newcomer Youth
events.description: Join our free Summer Camp for newcomer youth ages 14-18.  Participants should expect: Art and creative activities, Employment workshops, Field trips and volunteer hours.  Snacks, refreshments and Lunch are provided. Questions? Contact Nasim: 647-332-5711 or email nzand@nych.ca In partnership with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, North York Community House & Toronto Public Library. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children age 9+. events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Digital Photography I: The Basics

events.title: Japanese Friendship Bracelet Making for Kids!

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens . events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Control Systems This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs.
